title: oritrophium peruvianum subsp. lineatum
intro: Oritrophium peruvianum subsp. lineatum, also known as the 'Peruvian oriotrophia' plant, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its unique and striking appearance. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Oritrophium peruvianum subsp. lineatum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
